title: Zika virus - NHS
text:
tner has returned after travelling alone continue to use condoms for 2 months if you're female and you've returned from a country with a Zika virus risk after travelling alone If you or your partner are already pregnant, use condoms during and after your visit to a country with a Zika virus risk and for the rest of your pregnancy.
Speak to your midwife or doctor for advice if you're worried your unborn baby may be affected by Zika virus.
Guillain-Barré syndrome (GBS) Rarely, Zika virus can cause Guillain-Barré syndrome (GBS) , a serious condition that affects the nervous system.
Anyone can get Guillain-Barré syndrome, but it mainly affects adults and older children.
Immediate action required: Call 999 or go to A&E if:
You've recently travelled to a country with a Zika virus risk and:
you have numbness, pins and needles, muscle weakness or pain in your feet and hands that spreads to your arms and legs 
Information:
